1. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 12/19/24, 5:18 PM
    
    Long tables in the grand hall of Nightmarch’s Old Fortress overflow with roasted game, fresh bread, and starberry pies—the pride of Freehaven. Banners of the seven towns and the Nomen Centaurs hang alongside the central flag of Freehaven, a bold symbol of unity. Laughter rings through the air, mingling with the clink of mead-filled mugs and the lively tunes of bards. Outside, under a canopy of stars, a bonfire crackles, drawing villagers, lizardfolk, kobolds, dwarves, halflings, and centaurs into its glow. The Nomen Centaurs stand tall by the fire, their painted hides catching the flicker of flames. Leaders from across the kingdom exchange tales of struggle and triumph, their voices weaving the story of Freehaven. All of the party and their friends are in attendance: Riven, Astor, Lucas, Benedin, Pavel, Milo, Lee, Linzi, Harim, Octavia, Amiri, and many more. As the night deepens, Riven rises from his seat. Draped in his familiar green cloak and the sigil of Freehaven, his longbow resting beside him, he stands tall with quiet strength. Brumble, stretched out by the fire, lets out a deep, contented growl, sparking laughter before the hall falls silent.
    
2. [](https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/1319217242210177044/1319217285595922452/fixedpoint_A_grand_medieval_feast_in_a_large_stone_hall_illumin_2352035d-d259-41bd-b3da-99f0bd2a7d89-1.webp?ex=6a1de916&is=6a1c9796&hm=f6d01c2315c57c535b8e6230391ba35d6b51b5cecaec331ab220f97836ed5bd3&)
    
    ![Image](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/1319217242210177044/1319217285595922452/fixedpoint_A_grand_medieval_feast_in_a_large_stone_hall_illumin_2352035d-d259-41bd-b3da-99f0bd2a7d89-1.webp?ex=6a1de916&is=6a1c9796&hm=f6d01c2315c57c535b8e6230391ba35d6b51b5cecaec331ab220f97836ed5bd3&=&format=webp&width=700&height=700)![](data:image/png;base64,)
    
3. "Tonight, we celebrate more than victory—we honor what it means. When we stood against Vordekai, we stood against fear, against division, and we prevailed. Freehaven stands tall because of all of us—together, united. "From the forests of Nightmarch to the lakes of Silverstep, from Leveton’s markets to Varnhold’s streets, we’ve proven the strength of unity. Our allies—Nomen Centaurs, the people of Mivon, and others"—Riven nods subtly toward a hooded figure—"remind us that the dream of freedom knows no borders." "Let this feast be our promise: Freehaven will remain a beacon of liberty, hope, and strength. Together, we will face whatever comes, as one people, unbowed." He lifts his cup high: "To Freehaven—to freedom, to hope, and to all who call it home! And to allies old and new!" Cheers erupt, echoing through the hall and into the night.
    
4. @Danny @Darren [Pavel] @Slava [Astor] @Jon [Lucas] @Tom [Lee] @Billy [Milo]
    
5. Wes [Riven] changed the channel name: **14. The Great Feast of Freehaven** _—_ 12/20/24, 9:45 AM
    
6. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 12/20/24, 10:02 AM
    
    Pavel drinks grumpily, grateful that nobody is asking **him** to give a speech. He approves of the speech Riven gave, of course -- very flowery, full of fine and noble thoughts like Riven likes to have. But for Pavel, the noise and crowds and sheer **presence** of so many people is nearly painful. What if somebody came up to him and tried to engage in small talk? He reaches down to reassure himself that his Pavel Stick is still leaning against his leg, ready for use against any uncomfortable social interaction. He wasn't here by choice, of course. Riven had given him an assignment, so that was that. Pavel's duty is to guard their hooded guest. Or rather, to guard their hooded guest's **identity** more than the guest's actual personage. Although, if it came to that, Pavel figures he could make a pretty good bodyguard too.  
    _What's the point of the hood, anyway?_ Pavel wonders to himself. _That seems like it would draw attention rather than divert it._ After all, their guest was already glamoured; that ought to be enough. But Lucas had explained it with logic that was beyond the mind of a simple man: "You see, Pavel, the best way to keep a secret is to bury it inside a more obvious secret, so that if somebody penetrates the first mystery, they think they've uncovered all there is to know." Pavel snorts. The more he drinks, the more that starts to make sense. Cheers erupt again as Linzi stands on a tall stool to perform a dramatic reading. Pavel winces.
    
7. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 12/22/24, 5:23 AM
    
    Astor is glad to have everyone under one roof again. Whenever the group returns to govern, he seems stressed with an ever growing list of problems he presents in council. He's even gotten a small pair of reading glasses for a problem he didn't know he had, never having had to read anything that closely. But tonight's a feast, and that's one of his strengths. He's among those telling the tallest stories and laughing the loudest belly laughs. He challenges Amiri to a drinking rematch, and even invites those trying to engage Pavel in conversation to his circle instead.
    
8. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 12/23/24, 5:35 PM
    
    Riven, never as naturally gregarious as Astor, moves through the crowd with practiced charm. Over the years, he’s learned a thing or two about diplomacy, and tonight, he leans on those lessons. He shares stories when prompted, showcases artifacts from his travels, and, most importantly, listens—truly listens—to what lies in the hearts of his people. He wears a broad grin and a brave face, but those who know him best can see the strain. This is as much a battle for him as facing down a lich, and he’s determined to see it through with the same grit and resolve. After making his rounds, Riven joins Astor, catching his friend in a rare moment of calm between tall tales and challenges. Pouring Astor another round of the dwarven ale they’d uncovered in Varnhold, he watches him drain the mug before speaking, his gaze sweeping over the gathered crowd with quiet fondness. "You know, Vordekai nearly had us," Riven says softly. "If it weren’t for Lucas’s quick thinking, I’m not sure we’d all be here tonight." He pauses, then continues, his tone more serious. "I know you have your concerns about our adventures, and you’re not wrong. But confronting that creature was the right call. His eye would’ve turned to Freehaven soon enough." Riven hesitates, the weight of his words pressing down. "And I’ve learned something else. Narissa is behind it all—the Stag Lord, Hargulka, the Owlbear, the Cult of the Bloom, even Vordekai. This isn’t over, Astor. She won’t stop until we stop her. And I have to see it through—for the kingdom."
    
9. Astor’s reaction is immediate, and Riven holds up a hand to forestall him. "Before you say anything, hear me out. I know the risks. One day, I may not return. I’ve thought about it, and I’ve made my peace. But if that day comes, I have two requests." From within his cloak, Riven pulls out a letter, the parchment worn but carefully sealed. "First, send this to Kyonin." He holds it out, his voice steady though his eyes betray a flicker of vulnerability. "I hope it’s never read, but if it is, it might mend what I should have done myself." Astor glances at the envelope, noting the name written in flowing Elven script: Lyriel Anarindë. Riven’s gaze returns to the crowd, his expression softening with a rare mix of love and concern. "Second, lead these people. I trust you to do it. You’ve got the heart for it, and they’ll follow you." He pauses, letting the weight of his words settle, then adds, "I’ve prepared an official document with the scribes. It’s all detailed, but the people will need you, Astor." For a moment, the noise of the feast seems distant as Riven meets Astor’s gaze, the gravity of his words sinking in. Then, with a faint smile, he claps his friend on the shoulder. "But let’s hope none of that’s needed. For now, let’s enjoy the night." The two sit in companionable silence, the warmth of the fire and the sounds of celebration surrounding them, as if the weight of tomorrow can wait—just for tonight.
    
10. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 12/25/24, 7:45 AM
    
    --before the silence-- _Astor is surprised to hear this all from Riven, but he can tell the elf will brook no argument. He sighs, and leans back, relaxing into his chair._ **"You know, my family's motto is *'Endurance overcomes All'. I used to think it was just a fancy way of telling us to be tough."** _He lingers on the thought, laughing softly at his folly._ **"Guess it could be a warning too. When your enemy is a lich lord from thousands of years ago, he has the advantage in enduring. Bringing the fight to him was the right call. Bringing the fight to this immortal fey, Narissa? Makes sense to me too. Just wish we understood why she's meddling in all this. Maybe that's impossible, but maybe we could understand her better..."** **"Bah. Not like she's sent any letters. Just that calling card of hers, but no means to call."** **"Yuck, matters of state. Even at this feast it feels like there's work waiting. What I meant to say is, I think the motto is about safeguarding the next generation. So long as they continue, we continue. What we don't overcome, they will. So long as we give them that chance."** _Astor claps a hand to Riven's shoulder._ **"I'll keep your letter, and with a little luck, I'll be returning it to you on MY deathbed, how about that?"** _He laughs heartily. Riven can tell through their camaraderie that Astor understands the weight that he feels, but the large man seems to think everything will be alright._
    
11. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 12/25/24, 3:26 PM
    
    As the feast carries on, another would-be champion spots Astor and challenges him to an arm wrestle. Riven watches with a knowing smile, certain that Astor will win—but not before giving the young man from Serenko a glimmer of hope that he might have a chance. Moving through the crowd, Riven exchanges pleasantries with dignitaries and old friends. The merchant guild toasts loudly to six months of economic boom brought on by Freehaven’s policies. Cassandra intercepts him, urging him to visit Silverstep again. "The Silverstep Guard have developed new techniques with their glaives," she says proudly. "You’d enjoy seeing their progress." He nods, promising to visit soon, and continues weaving through the lively hall. Spotting Octavia, he pauses. "Octavia, thank you—for everything you’ve done for Freehaven. Your magic, your heart… this kingdom wouldn’t be the same without you." She grins, looking uncharacteristically bashful. "You’re too kind, Riven, but thank you. That means a lot, but something is clearly on your mind." Riven meets her gaze, his tone warm but purposeful. "I’d like to offer you something more: the mayorship of Varnhold. The town needs a leader with your spirit and wisdom." Octavia blinks, caught off guard. "Mayor? Me? I… I don’t know. I’ll need to think about it." Riven nods, his expression understanding. "Take your time. Let me know when you’re ready. I only ask that you do well by the people and continue to improve relations with the Nomen Centaurs." As the revelry continues, Riven quietly slips away from the center of the hall. He moves unnoticed through the crowd, the noise of the feast fading a bit. In a shadowed corner, he finds Pavel sitting watchfully. Without preamble, Riven speaks in a hushed tone. "How is our guest?"
    
12. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 12/28/24, 10:20 AM
    
    Pavel blows air out his mouth exaggeratedly. "Honestly? I don't know. She asked about Cat, but she got this super quiet and far-away look in her eyes when I was only halfway through explaining the variation in paw prints between the 10 different species of jaguars known to inhabit the river kingdoms. Most people look that way when they see someone they know across the room and have to go greet them, but of course, she doesn't know anyone here, so that wasn't it. But I stopped after the 10th species and she never reacted. Her eyes are closed. Do you think she might be praying?" "Quite likely," says Riven, straight-faced. Riven taps his mug companionably against Pavel's, and they sit in silence for a few minutes. As usual, Pavel sits still, with no fidgeting or wasted motion of any kind. He appears focused on a group of young people gathered in a tight knot across the hall, where Astor is effortlessly holding court. To Riven, the briefest flicker in Pavel's eyes gives away the true object of his interest, as Edina Aldori, visiting from Restov, trades verbal jabs and barbs with Astor. The group's laughter echoes across the hall, at which Pavel's mouth twitches in the barest grimace.
    
13. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 1/5/25, 7:28 AM
    
    Riven catches Pavel’s glance toward Edina and leans in slightly. “Pavel, I’ll keep an eye on our guest. But it looks like Edina could use some looking after. Why don’t you take her on a walk? Show her some of the artifacts from our travels—she might actually enjoy hearing about the jaguars.”
    
14. ### Danny _—_ 1/7/25, 6:40 AM
    
    In the corner, the hooded figure silently shakes her head in disbelief for at least the dozenth time tonight. How could she have missed how successful the "motley six" had been? It's true that they were instrumental in saving her estate during the disastrous events on the evening of the stolen lands chartering feast, but she had always assumed that they were just good in a fight, and might struggle to rule. She always knew that all the charterholders were going to have a rough time in the stolen lands, but Freehaven and the six seemed to attract more trouble than even she would have thought possible. Despite their growth, she thought their knack for finding a fight would get the best of their kingdom eventually. Her money--and her eye--had always been on Maegar. He seemed to have the right temperament of a ruler, and a strategic marriage with him could have strengthened Restov. But now he was dead and Varnhold in tatters, the Iron Wraiths had gone dark and were assumed dead and who knew what was up with Hannis Drevlev? The reports were not good… Yes, the six were good in a fight, but who could have foreseen their success in diplomacy? Even the Nomen were here! They have always spurned everyone. Also, who could have guessed the six would develop a happy populace from among the rough-and-tumble occupants of the stolen lands? And what of their economy?! She was still amazed at the thriving city of Freehaven. It rivaled even Restov! What it lacked in old history and charm it made up for in pure energy and commerce.
    
15. She had been too distracted by the intrigue with Brevoy. She had entirely missed the significance of what was going on down here. Thank heavens they were still friendly -- she still had a chance to strengthen ties with them. Developing these ties might even be more important than fighting the infuriating political battles up north. And yet, there was still so much that could go wrong… There were worrying whispers from the south about Irovetti. Should she warn these leaders, or would that just stir the pot? Heck, if they can make friends with the Nomen, maybe even Irovetti will fall for their charms. Well, probably not… But most of all, this news of "Nyrissa" wreaking havoc is deeply disturbing. Anyone with the power to bring back a monster like Vordekai could pose a threat to Restov as well, and she had no idea where to start to try to get her arms around the problem -- except to draw closer to these six. Could her own kingdom's salvation eventually end up in the hands of the "motley six"? Unthinkable! She catches herself shaking her head in disbelief again. Who would have guessed it all would have gone like this?
    
16. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 1/8/25, 8:18 AM
    
    Riven approaches the hooded figure, his voice low but steady amidst the celebration. "Lady Jamandi, I’m glad you came. You took a chance on us that night years ago, and look what’s become of it. None of this would’ve been possible without your faith in us." He places a mug in front of her with a faint smile. "You must try our new dwarven ale. Benedin told me the name, but I’ve never been much for dwarven tongues. To old friends and enduring alliances." He raises his mug, his tone warm but measured. His gaze sweeps the room briefly before returning to her. "This hall is full of stories—settlers from our towns, kobolds, lizardfolk, centaurs, even Mivon. And now you, here among us. It feels like the beginning of something greater." Riven’s tone becomes thoughtful. "Restov was the catalyst for change in these lands. I know you carry heavy burdens there. But I can’t help but wonder—where do you see us, Freehaven and Restov, in the days ahead? What role will you play in the stories yet to come?" @Danny
    
17. ### Danny _—_ 1/9/25, 7:26 AM
    
    Lady Jamandi hesitates a moment and says, “I had hoped to have that conversation with you. I’d like to explore ways to forge closer ties. With the dangers rising, a strong alliance will be beneficial to us both. However, it will behoove both of us to do this in a way that doesn’t stir up the suspicions and wrath of some of the more reactionary minds in the north. There is strong suspicion there about Restov becoming more aligned with kingdoms to the south. I think it is entirely unfounded, but it is hard to reason with some.”
    
18. I do understand their concerns about Pitax, but they needn’t worry about me in that regard. I keep that relationship at a cordial but cautious arms length
    
19. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 1/10/25, 3:34 AM
    
    Riven listens closely, his expression calm but intent. After a moment, he speaks, his tone thoughtful and grounded. "You’re right—strengthening ties requires care. But Freehaven and Restov can be more than allies; we can be an example of unity in uncertain times. Small steps—traders, craftsmen, even shared patrols—could build trust without stirring unrest. When the moment comes, we’ll be ready for something greater." He pauses, his gaze steady. "I’ve heard the whispers about Irovetti, but whispers aren’t enough. What’s his reach, truly? And in Brevoy, where do the real dangers lie? The more I understand, the better I can protect my people—and work with yours."
    
20. @Danny
    
21. ### Danny _—_ 1/15/25, 7:43 AM
    
    "It isn't Irovetti's current reach that worries me, it is his ambition. He is no ordinary man. He came from out of nowhere, with no name or history, and suddenly there was a signed document handing over the kingdom to him. No one seems to have all the details of his rise to power, but my agents finally unlocked a key secret.
    
22. "They found out that he hasn't always gone by the name of Castruccio Irovetti. He was born an illegitimate son to a father he never met. By his early twenties, he lost his mother and was orphaned. They believe his name was Mandalarucio. The trail gets cloudy, but he has had some run-ins with the Technic League and that is possibly why he changed his name. The only other thing we know about his past is that he somehow got hold of some very powerful devices -- perhaps from the Technic League. It is thought that he has used those devices in his rise to power.
    
23. "In any case, he is a man who has risen from nothing to the King of Pitax with a very mysterious, shady past. He scares me."
    
24. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 1/15/25, 5:00 PM
    
    Riven takes a slow breath, his expression unreadable as long-buried memories surface. His voice is measured, steady. "Irovetti’s ambition isn’t new to me. He thrives in shadows, manipulating others. His rise left scars on more than just his enemies." He taps the side of his mug, his tone dropping. "The Technic League’s artifacts in his hands are tools that could topple kingdoms. If his gaze turns toward Freehaven—or Restov—we’ll need to stand together. Clever as he is, he’s not invincible." Riven meets her gaze, a guarded smile flickering. "It’s good to know you’re watching. Men like Irovetti don’t offer second chances."
    
25. ### Wes [Riven] _—_ 2/1/25, 6:47 AM
    
    As the night wore on, Riven left the visitor and made his way toward the great hearth. He raised a hand, but the revelry continued unchecked. Ekundayo nudged Brumble with his foot, and the great bear let out a deep roar that silenced the hall. With an easy leap, Riven landed atop a table, stepping between tankards and platters without spilling a drop. He spread his arms. "I trust you’ve all enjoyed the feast!" A voice called out, "And it shouldn’t stop!" The crowd banged their mugs on tables in agreement. "No, it shouldn’t," Riven said with a grin. "And it won’t. But before you drink yourselves past remembering, I have an announcement." "Too late!" someone jeered, drawing raucous laughter. "Fair point," Riven conceded, "but listen well." He let the moment settle before continuing. "Three months from now, Freehaven will host a grand tournament. A team tournament. Anyone from Freehaven or beyond may enter, but you’ll need a team." He let the words sink in, then continued. "Five events will test your mettle: a battle of bards, a drinking and eating contest, a labyrinth escape, a monster hunt, and, of course, an archery contest." He scanned the hall, his voice carrying over the crackling fire and eager murmurs. "So bring the best team you can muster. There will be competition. There will be prizes. And there will be glory!" The hall erupted in cheers, mugs slamming against tables. Riven raised his own, a glint of amusement in his eyes. "Now, let the night carry on!"
